title: Tapping to Love Saving and Investing
subtitle: A tapping practice for releasing fear around saving and putting money to work
excerpt: A five-minute tapping practice from Day 52 of a 12-week money program. The script works with the belief that saving means restriction and that investing is too complicated or risky to attempt. The reframe moves toward saving and investing as satisfying, active parts of wealth building.
sourceNotes: The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), in use since the mid-1990s. The script for this practice is adapted from Day 52 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program to Attract Cash, Overflow & Abundance Without Hustle or Burnout (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798268793796).

practice.whenToUse: Use when you feel resistance to setting money aside for savings or when the idea of investing feels out of reach.

content.text: A five-minute tapping practice from Day 52 of a 12-week money program. The script targets the belief that saving takes something away and that investing is too complex or frightening to touch. The reframe moves toward growing money as something that feels active and good.
New to tapping? Read
how EFT tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop
Even though saving and investing once felt like chores, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I worried I'd do it wrong, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I thought it meant restriction, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Tapping round
Eyebrow: I've seen saving as taking something away.
Side of eye: I thought investing was too complex.
Under eye: I doubted I could grow money wisely.
Under nose: I feared losing what I saved.
Chin: I avoided big financial moves.
Collarbone: I'm ready to welcome these habits.
Under arm: I want saving and investing to feel exciting.
Top of head: I choose to enjoy this part of wealth.
Reframe to positive
Eyebrow: I love watching savings rise.
Side of eye: I invest with calm confidence.
Under eye: I see money multiplying over time.
Under nose: I enjoy putting wealth to work for me.
Chin: I celebrate every pound saved and invested.
Collarbone: I feel capable of growing money with wisdom.
Under arm: I trust myself to grow money steadily.
Top of head: I enjoy saving and investing as part of my life.
Tap each round three times, then move to the reframe and tap that three times.
Where this practice comes from
The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), developed in the mid-1990s. The script is adapted from Day 52 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
